clues: A user accessing a shared network drive sees files from the wrong department's folder.
The drive letter mapping shows the same server address as always.
Other users in the same department access the correct folder without problems.
The issue started after the IT team reorganized the file server's folder structure.
The user's mapped drive points to the server address with an old share name that was repurposed.
